matter_name: Al Nakheel Logistics Group — SPA Review
key_legal_issues: Seller warranty on fleet ownership is incomplete — 14 vehicles are registered in the personal name of the founder rather than the company, requiring title transfer before closing
Material adverse change clause as drafted does not exclude industry-wide logistics disruptions — buyer requested carve-out for GCC geopolitical events is under negotiation
Non-compete covenant of 36 months covers all of Kuwait, KSA, and UAE — likely unenforceable as drafted under ADGM Employment Regulations if founder is also an employee post-close
Completion accounts mechanism uses Kuwait GAAP rather than IFRS — adjustment methodology is disputed between parties' accountants
recommended_action: Require vehicle title transfer to company as condition precedent; revise non-compete to Kuwait only for 24 months to ensure enforceability under ADGM standards.
estimated_resolution_timeline: 45–60 days to closing subject to title transfer completion

matter_name: Rawabi Medical Centers — NHIF Dispute
key_legal_issues: NHIF has unilaterally reduced reimbursement rates for 6 chronic disease management codes effective retroactively — Rawabi claims SAR 2.8M in underpayments
NHIF disputes that the retroactive rate reduction constitutes a breach of the framework agreement — NHIF asserts regulatory authority to revise tariffs without consent
Rawabi's framework agreement does not contain a tariff stability clause — absence of contractual protection weakens the commercial claim
Administrative appeal to NHIF disputes committee was rejected — commercial court is the next available forum
recommended_action: File urgent commercial court application for interim injunction to prevent further retroactive deductions pending full hearing; simultaneously engage NHIF through Ministry of Health escalation channel.
estimated_resolution_timeline: 12–18 months for commercial court judgment; 6 months for potential MOHP administrative resolution

matter_name: Salam Printing — Founder Succession Transfer
key_legal_issues: Kuwait Companies Law requires shareholder approval by 75% majority for share transfer in a WLL — minority shareholder holding 20% has not confirmed approval, creating closing risk
Founder's personal guarantee on KWD 380K bank facility with NBK must be released before closing — NBK has not confirmed release timeline
Three of six printing equipment items are subject to lease agreements that contain change-of-control clauses requiring lessor consent
Employment contracts for 14 Kuwaiti staff contain provisions referencing the founder personally — novation to new owner required under Kuwait Labour Law
recommended_action: Obtain notarized minority shareholder approval immediately; engage NBK for personal guarantee release letter as a condition precedent to closing; novate all employment contracts at closing.
estimated_resolution_timeline: 30–45 days subject to NBK cooperation

matter_name: Tamayuz Education Group — MOE Fee Dispute
key_legal_issues: MOE rejected Tamayuz's 8% annual fee increase application for the 2024–2025 academic year, approving only 3% — SAR 12M revenue impact over the school year
MOE's fee approval framework does not provide a right of appeal within the administrative structure — commercial court jurisdiction is disputed by MOE
Tamayuz's operating cost submissions to MOE were prepared without IFRS-compliant financial statements — MOE has cited this as grounds for the reduced approval
NSPK private school licensing terms do not include an explicit fee stabilization guarantee — contractual basis for the claim is weak
recommended_action: Prepare IFRS-compliant cost submission for resubmission to MOE before next academic year review; engage specialist education regulatory counsel to assess administrative court jurisdiction before commercial court filing.
estimated_resolution_timeline: 18–24 months for full resolution; administrative resubmission outcome within 90 days

matter_name: Gulf Star Food Industries — Distribution Agreement Dispute
key_legal_issues: Al Baraka claims Gulf Star breached exclusivity provisions by supplying a Sharjah retailer outside the designated distribution territory
Gulf Star disputes that the Sharjah retailer falls within Al Baraka's exclusive territory — the distribution agreement uses ambiguous geographic descriptions that do not reference Emirate boundaries
Al Baraka is claiming SAR 4.2M in lost profits — causation between the alleged breach and lost sales is disputed and requires expert evidence
The distribution agreement was executed in 2019 and has not been renewed — Al Baraka argues it remains in force by conduct while Gulf Star contends it expired
recommended_action: Commission independent expert report on territorial boundary interpretation before filing defense; assess settlement exposure at AED 800K–1.4M to avoid 18-month arbitration process.
estimated_resolution_timeline: 18–24 months for full DIFC arbitration; 3–6 months for mediated settlement

matter_name: Al Jazeera Energy Services — KOC Contract Renewal
key_legal_issues: KOC has issued a framework agreement renewal with a 12% reduction in unit rates — Al Jazeera disputes the basis for rate reduction given unchanged scope and cost inflation
KOC's standard contract terms include a unilateral rate revision clause that Al Jazeera signed in 2019 — enforceability of this clause under Kuwait Civil Law is uncertain given its breadth
KOC has imposed new ESG reporting requirements in the renewed framework — compliance cost is estimated at KWD 240K annually and was not flagged during negotiations
KOC procurement manager rotation has changed the primary relationship contact — new manager is enforcing contract terms more strictly than predecessor
recommended_action: Seek direct meeting between Al Jazeera CEO and KOC Procurement Director to negotiate rate reduction to 6% with ESG cost reimbursement mechanism — avoid formal dispute given KOC relationship dependency.
estimated_resolution_timeline: 60–90 days through commercial negotiation; avoid litigation given KOC contract dependency

matter_name: Nakheel Urban Mobility — RTA Permit Dispute
key_legal_issues: RTA has issued a notice of permit breach following 3 rider incidents in restricted zones — Nakheel disputes that the incidents occurred within restricted zones based on GPS data
RTA's permit terms include a strict liability provision for zone violations regardless of rider fault — Nakheel's GPS evidence may not override this contractual provision
RTA has indicated it may suspend the exclusive zone permit for 30 days pending investigation — permit suspension would halt 100% of operations and trigger investor default clauses
Nakheel's Series A term sheet includes a regulatory breach default provision giving investors redemption rights if permit is suspended for more than 14 days
recommended_action: Immediately engage RTA through formal written response with GPS evidence; retain UAE administrative law counsel to challenge strict liability interpretation; notify investors proactively to manage default clause risk.
estimated_resolution_timeline: 30–45 days for RTA investigation; suspension risk within 14 days if not resolved

matter_name: Murooj Technology Solutions — Ministry Contract Dispute
key_legal_issues: Ministry of Finance has withheld KWD 420K in invoices for ERP customization work claiming deliverables did not meet technical specifications — Murooj disputes the specification interpretation
Ministry's technical review committee used evaluation criteria not included in the original SOW — Murooj argues the criteria were introduced post-delivery
Government contract dispute resolution under Kuwait law requires mandatory internal ministry committee review before court access — Murooj has not completed this step
Payment delay of 280 days has caused Murooj's credit facility to be drawn to 94% — working capital crisis is imminent if not resolved within 45 days
recommended_action: File formal ministry committee dispute application immediately to preserve court rights; simultaneously engage Ministry procurement director for commercial resolution given working capital urgency.
estimated_resolution_timeline: 6–9 months for ministry committee process; 18–24 months if escalated to commercial court

matter_name: Barr Al Aman Development — Zoning Challenge

parties.applicant: Barr Al Aman Real Estate Development Co.

key_legal_issues: Jeddah Municipality amended the zoning classification of two Barr Al Aman land plots from residential to mixed-use commercial, halting SAR 38M in planned residential development
The zoning amendment was issued without the mandatory 30-day public notice period required under the Saudi Urban Planning Law — procedural breach is documentable
Barr Al Aman has construction permits already issued on the basis of prior residential zoning — validity of issued permits after zoning change is disputed
The land plots were acquired based on a municipality pre-approval letter that confirmed residential zoning — Barr Al Aman has a reliance argument on municipality's own representations
recommended_action: File administrative court challenge to zoning amendment based on procedural breach of notice period; preserve construction permits validity through immediate submission of appeal to Municipality technical committee.
estimated_resolution_timeline: 12–18 months for administrative court judgment; interim permit suspension risk within 60 days

matter_name: Thuraya Medical Supplies — Exclusive Distribution Termination
key_legal_issues: German manufacturer Medika has served notice of termination of exclusive distribution agreement citing non-performance of minimum purchase obligations in 2023
Thuraya argues the minimum purchase shortfall was caused by MOH budget freeze in Q3 2023 — a force majeure event not covered by the agreement's limited force majeure clause
Medika's termination notice period is 6 months — during this period Thuraya is seeking injunctive relief to prevent Medika from appointing an alternative Kuwait distributor
The distribution agreement is governed by German law — Thuraya's Kuwait counsel is not qualified to advise on German law interpretation, requiring German co-counsel
recommended_action: Retain German co-counsel immediately to assess force majeure argument under German law; file urgent ICC arbitration application for interim relief preventing alternative distributor appointment.
estimated_resolution_timeline: ICC interim relief: 30 days; full arbitration: 18–24 months

matter_name: Rimal Construction Group — Subcontractor Dispute
key_legal_issues: Al Muqrin is claiming SAR 8.4M for work completed on 3 Rimal sites alleging non-payment beyond 90 days — Rimal disputes the claim quantum by SAR 2.1M citing defective work deductions
Rimal applied backcharge deductions without serving the contractually required 14-day defect notice — procedural breach weakens Rimal's counterclaim
Al Muqrin has filed a labor complaint with HRSD against Rimal for delayed worker wage payments — this parallel proceeding creates additional regulatory risk
Two of three disputed sites are REDF-funded projects subject to REDF audit — any commercial court judgment against Rimal would trigger REDF vendor review
recommended_action: Resolve HRSD labor complaint immediately through direct payment to prevent regulatory escalation; settle commercial dispute at SAR 5.8M to avoid REDF vendor review trigger.
estimated_resolution_timeline: HRSD complaint: 30 days; commercial settlement: 60–90 days

matter_name: Mashaer Transport Group — MOI Permit Lapse
key_legal_issues: MOI has issued a SAR 480K fine for operating 28 buses with expired vehicle registration during a 14-day permit renewal delay — Mashaer disputes the fine calculation methodology
MOI enforcement action includes a 90-day ban on new bus fleet registrations — Mashaer needs 40 new bus registrations for the upcoming Hajj season contract
The permit lapse was caused by an MOI portal system outage during the renewal window — Mashaer has documentary evidence of the outage but MOI has not accepted force majeure
Hajj season contract with Ministry of Hajj includes a compliance warranty — the MOI fine may constitute a breach of this warranty triggering penalty clauses
recommended_action: File formal administrative appeal with MOI Technical Committee with documented evidence of portal outage; simultaneously engage Ministry of Hajj to seek waiver of compliance warranty breach pending MOI resolution.
estimated_resolution_timeline: MOI administrative appeal: 45–60 days; Hajj Ministry engagement: immediate

matter_name: Layali Hospitality Group — Franchise Agreement Review
key_legal_issues: Master franchise agreement requires Layali to open 3 additional franchise locations in Kuwait within 24 months — Layali has opened only 1 due to mall leasing delays
Mena Dining Brands is claiming material breach and seeking to terminate the Bahrain expansion exclusivity that was conditional on Kuwait performance
Kuwait shopping mall landlords have imposed build-out specifications that conflict with the franchisor's brand standards — Layali argues this constitutes a force majeure event
Franchise royalty calculation methodology has been disputed for 3 years — Layali claims AED 280K in royalty overpayments; Mena Dining disputes this calculation
recommended_action: Negotiate a 12-month extension on Kuwait expansion timeline with modified performance milestones; resolve royalty dispute through agreed independent accountant review.
estimated_resolution_timeline: 90 days for negotiated extension; 12 months for royalty dispute if not settled

matter_name: Majd Pharmaceutical Manufacturing — SFDA GMP Finding
key_legal_issues: SFDA GMP inspection found 3 critical observations and 7 major observations — Majd has 30 days to submit a corrective action plan or face production suspension for 4 product lines
One critical observation relates to batch release testing methodology — SFDA is questioning whether 12 batches released in 2023 met specification and may require product recall
SFDA's recall authority under the Food and Drug Law is broad and can be exercised without judicial process — Majd's ability to challenge a recall notice in real time is limited
Two of the 4 product lines at risk supply the MOH — any production suspension triggers MOH supply contract force majeure provisions
recommended_action: Submit comprehensive CAPA within 21 days retaining SFDA-accredited regulatory consultant; proactively notify MOH of potential supply disruption to manage relationship before formal SFDA action.
estimated_resolution_timeline: CAPA review: 45 days; potential production suspension: risk within 30 days if CAPA inadequate

matter_name: Bader Insurance Brokers — CMA License Renewal
key_legal_issues: Kuwait CMA has queried Bader's fitness and propriety documentation during license renewal — the founder's 2008 regulatory censure in Bahrain has surfaced in the CMA due diligence
CMA regulations require disclosure of all prior regulatory actions in all jurisdictions — the 2008 Bahrain censure was not disclosed in the original 2012 Kuwait license application
CMA is considering whether the non-disclosure constitutes material misrepresentation justifying license suspension pending investigation
Bader's 340 corporate clients have insurance policies that require a licensed broker — any license suspension would trigger mass policy transfer to competitor brokers
recommended_action: Engage Kuwait CMA through formal voluntary disclosure letter acknowledging the 2008 Bahrain matter and providing full context; retain CMA-specialist regulatory counsel immediately before CMA issues formal inquiry.
estimated_resolution_timeline: CMA investigation: 90–180 days; suspension risk: material if proactive disclosure not made within 14 days

matter_name: Imdad Supply Chain Solutions — Technology Platform IP Dispute
key_legal_issues: Former technology vendor LogiTech is claiming ownership of the proprietary supply chain platform developed during a 3-year development engagement — LogiTech argues the IP assignment clause in the 2016 services agreement was not effective

Imdad's legal position is that the 2019 addendum to the services agreement clearly assigned all IP to Imdad — LogiTech disputes the addendum's authority as it was signed by a project manager, not an authorized signatory
LogiTech has registered a UAE copyright for the platform software modules as a defensive measure — Imdad's ability to freely rebuild the platform is now constrained pending resolution
Imdad's planned AED 8M platform rebuild will replicate certain elements of the disputed software — LogiTech has issued a cease and desist for any rebuild activity using the disputed codebase
recommended_action: Commission independent IP counsel to assess validity of LogiTech's copyright registration; structure platform rebuild using entirely new codebase to eliminate infringement risk while arbitration proceeds.
estimated_resolution_timeline: Interim injunction application: 30 days; full DIFC arbitration: 18–24 months

matter_name: Al Baraka Steel Fabrication — Minority Shareholder Challenge
key_legal_issues: Minority sibling holding 15% has filed a commercial court application challenging the sale price as unfair — he commissioned an independent valuation at SAR 280M against the agreed SAR 299M EV
Under Saudi Companies Law, a minority shareholder dissenting from a share sale can seek judicial valuation — the court-appointed valuer's process is unpredictable in timeline and methodology
The shareholder agreement does not contain a drag-along clause — buyer requires unanimous shareholder consent for the full share transfer to proceed
Minority shareholder is demanding a 22% price premium for his shares as the condition for his consent — this is not commercially justifiable given his lack of operational involvement
recommended_action: Negotiate a controlled premium of 8–10% for the minority stake to secure consent and avoid judicial valuation risk; engage tribal mediation channel through family elder to reach consensus outside court.
estimated_resolution_timeline: Mediated resolution: 60–90 days; court valuation process: 12–18 months

matter_name: Durrat Al Khaleej Real Estate — Tenant Default

parties.landlord: Durrat Al Khaleej Real Estate Co. WLL
parties.tenant: Al Watan Financial Services Co. KSCC

key_legal_issues: Al Watan Financial Services has defaulted on 4 months of office rent totalling KWD 68,000 and is disputing the lease renewal terms
Al Watan has counterclaimed KWD 24,000 for alleged defective air conditioning that caused equipment damage — the counterclaim is being used as a set-off defense
Kuwait tenancy law provides significant protections for commercial tenants — eviction proceedings require a court order and minimum 6-month notice even for non-payment
Al Watan holds CMA-licensed financial services operations in the premises — eviction creates a risk of CMA licensing disruption that Al Watan is leveraging in negotiations
recommended_action: File urgent summary judgment application for KWD 44,000 net of counterclaim; simultaneously serve formal eviction notice to start 6-month clock; reject settlement below KWD 56,000.
estimated_resolution_timeline: Summary judgment: 90–120 days; eviction order: 9–12 months from filing

matter_name: Siraj Renewable Energy Solutions — DEWA Contractor Dispute
key_legal_issues: DEWA has refused to grant approved contractor status citing a technical non-compliance in Siraj's quality management system documentation submitted in 2022
Siraj re-submitted corrected documentation in 2023 — DEWA has not responded within the regulatory 90-day review period creating a deemed refusal under DEWA's own procedures
Without DEWA approval, Siraj cannot connect solar installations to the Dubai grid — AED 140M in LOI pipeline is at risk of cancellation
DEWA's contractor approval process has no formal appeal mechanism — judicial review is the only available remedy and is untested for DEWA contractor decisions
recommended_action: Escalate formally to DEWA CEO office with documentation of 90-day deemed refusal; simultaneously engage UAE Federal Courts for judicial review application to create procedural pressure on DEWA.
estimated_resolution_timeline: DEWA escalation: 45–60 days; judicial review: 12–18 months

matter_name: Noor Healthcare REIT — Lease Renegotiation Dispute
key_legal_issues: Gulf Medical Group is seeking to renegotiate its triple net lease terms citing COVID-19 related revenue impact — the lease contains no force majeure clause for pandemic events
Gulf Medical Group has withheld SAR 480K in rent citing alleged property maintenance failures by the REIT — the triple net structure explicitly assigns maintenance to the tenant
ADGM's REIT regulations require the fund to maintain minimum occupancy and rental income thresholds for regulatory compliance — any lease restructuring must be reported to FSRA
Gulf Medical Group's financial position has deteriorated — debt service coverage ratio is 0.9x against a lease covenant of 1.2x, triggering a lease default notice right
recommended_action: Issue formal default notice triggering 30-day cure period; offer controlled rent deferral of 15% for 12 months in exchange for lease extension and enhanced maintenance obligations; notify FSRA proactively.
estimated_resolution_timeline: Negotiated restructuring: 60 days; formal arbitration if not resolved: 18–24 months

matter_name: Kanz Wealth Technology — CBK Licensing Dispute
key_legal_issues: CBK has issued an informal query regarding whether Kanz's robo-advisory activities require a full investment management license rather than the lighter robo-advisory license held
Kanz's platform offers discretionary rebalancing features — CBK's position is that discretionary investment management without a full CMA license is a regulatory breach
The distinction between automated advisory and discretionary management is not clearly defined in Kuwait's 2021 fintech regulatory framework — interpretation is contested
CBK has informally indicated it may suspend the robo-advisory license pending formal review — 2,400 client accounts would need to be transferred to a licensed manager
recommended_action: Submit formal legal opinion from Kuwait regulatory counsel to CBK distinguishing automated rebalancing from discretionary management; request formal written ruling from CBK before any operational changes.
estimated_resolution_timeline: CBK informal review: 60–90 days; license suspension risk: material without proactive engagement

matter_name: Bayt Al Shifa Pharmacy Chain — SFDA Pricing Dispute

parties.party_a: Bayt Al Shifa Pharmacy Chain Co.

key_legal_issues: SFDA has issued a SAR 680K administrative fine for selling 8 prescription medications above the regulated maximum retail price — Bayt Al Shifa disputes the pricing violation for 5 of 8 products
The pricing violations relate to medications where the regulated price was updated in an SFDA circular not received by Bayt Al Shifa through the standard notification channel
SFDA's administrative fine appeal process requires suspension of operations at 2 branches pending appeal — Bayt Al Shifa disputes the branch suspension authority
NUPCO framework supply agreement may be at risk if SFDA fine is not resolved — NUPCO contract includes a clean regulatory record warranty
recommended_action: File administrative appeal for 5 disputed products with evidence of notification failure; negotiate immediate payment of undisputed 3-product fine to prevent branch suspension and NUPCO contract risk.
estimated_resolution_timeline: Administrative appeal: 90 days; branch suspension risk: within 30 days if not resolved

matter_name: Ruwad Facilities Management — ADNOC Contract Dispute
key_legal_issues: ADNOC has claimed AED 2.4M in performance deductions under the existing subcontract for failing to meet Emiratization KPIs — Ruwad disputes both the deduction amount and the KPI measurement methodology
The subcontract KPI framework was incorporated by reference to an ADNOC internal standard not shared with Ruwad at contract execution — Ruwad argues it cannot be bound by undisclosed standards
ADNOC has exercised a step-in right for 2 facility management teams — Ruwad disputes the step-in trigger conditions were met under the contract
Main FM contract tender for ADNOC campus is pending — any formal dispute with ADNOC could disqualify Ruwad from the tender evaluation
recommended_action: Resolve through direct ADNOC executive engagement — avoid formal dispute to protect tender eligibility; accept AED 1.2M settlement on deductions while formally objecting to step-in rights exercise in writing.
estimated_resolution_timeline: Commercial resolution: 45–60 days; formal arbitration: 18–24 months with tender disqualification risk

matter_name: Talal Food Processing — LuLu Supply Agreement Dispute
key_legal_issues: LuLu has unilaterally reduced its annual hummus purchase commitment by 35% citing shelf space reallocation — Talal claims this breaches the annual purchase commitment in the supply agreement
LuLu's supply agreement contains a shelf space allocation clause giving LuLu discretion to modify — Talal argues this clause does not override the minimum purchase commitment
Talal has 3 months of excess inventory accumulated on the basis of LuLu's original commitment — working capital impact is KWD 120K
The supply agreement renewal is due in 4 months — formal dispute would likely result in non-renewal, which would be more damaging than the current purchase reduction
recommended_action: Pursue commercial resolution through senior buyer relationship at LuLu; propose promotional program to move excess inventory before raising formal dispute — prioritize renewal over recovery of current shortfall.
estimated_resolution_timeline: Commercial resolution: 30–45 days; formal dispute inadvisable given renewal dependency

matter_name: Wajha Digital Identity Solutions — PDPL Compliance Review

parties.respondent: Saudi Data and Artificial Intelligence Authority

key_legal_issues: SDAIA has initiated a PDPL compliance audit following a complaint from a financial institution client regarding data retention practices

Wajha retains identity verification records for 7 years — PDPL Article 18 requires data deletion when the purpose for which data was collected is fulfilled, which SDAIA interprets as 90 days post-verification
1.2M identity verification records are at issue — SDAIA's potential remediation order could require mass deletion affecting Wajha's fraud detection analytics system
NCA separately regulates identity data under the Cybersecurity Framework — dual regulatory exposure creates coordination risk if SDAIA and NCA issue conflicting orders
recommended_action: Submit PDPL compliance representation to SDAIA proposing a 2-year data retention standard with documented legal basis; simultaneously align with NCA on acceptable retention period to prevent conflicting regulatory directions.
estimated_resolution_timeline: SDAIA audit response: 45 days; formal enforcement action risk: within 90 days if response inadequate

matter_name: Hayat Home Care Services — DHA Staff Credential Dispute

parties.party_b: Samer Khalil (former Head of Clinical Operations)

key_legal_issues: Former Head of Clinical Operations is claiming AED 380K in unpaid bonus and wrongful termination damages — termination was for cause citing undisclosed credential gap in his DHA registration
Hayat's termination for cause relies on the DHA credential gap — the gap was identified during a routine DHA audit, not through Hayat's own screening process, which weakens the for-cause argument

The employment contract was drafted under UAE Labour Law but the bonus agreement was a separate document governed by DIFC law — jurisdictional conflict between UAE Labour Court and DIFC Court is unresolved
DHA has separately issued a compliance notice to Hayat for failing to verify DHA credentials at the point of appointment — regulatory exposure is independent of the employment claim
recommended_action: Separate the DHA regulatory matter from the employment dispute; negotiate AED 180K settlement of employment claim to avoid DIFC court proceedings; address DHA compliance notice independently.
estimated_resolution_timeline: Employment settlement: 45 days; DHA compliance: 60 days

matter_name: Mazaya Digital Payments — CBK Provisional License Conditions
key_legal_issues: CBK provisional e-payment license includes a condition requiring a Kuwaiti banking institution as a settlement bank — Mazaya's preferred settlement bank NBK has declined the relationship citing AML risk appetite
Without a settlement bank, Mazaya's provisional license cannot convert to full license — CBK has set a 90-day deadline before provisional license is revoked
Mazaya's 1,400 merchant contracts contain a license continuity warranty — any license revocation would trigger immediate mass contract termination
CBK has informally indicated that Boubyan Bank may be willing to act as settlement bank — relationship introduction has not been facilitated despite Mazaya's requests
recommended_action: Request formal CBK facilitation of Boubyan Bank introduction; simultaneously approach Warba Bank and Kuwait International Bank as alternative settlement bank candidates; submit formal CBK extension request for the 90-day deadline.
estimated_resolution_timeline: Settlement bank resolution: 45–60 days; license revocation risk: within 90 days

matter_name: Qimam Aviation Services — GACA Concession Dispute
key_legal_issues: GACA has unilaterally introduced new environmental compliance requirements into the concession agreement without the contractually required mutual amendment process
The new environmental requirements will cost SAR 28M to implement — Qimam argues this is a material change to the concession economics that triggers a price adjustment mechanism

The concession agreement's price adjustment mechanism requires GACA's consent — GACA is refusing to trigger the mechanism claiming the environmental requirements are regulatory obligations outside the concession scope
Qimam's project finance lenders have a GACA concession compliance covenant — any formal dispute with GACA could trigger a technical default under the financing documents
recommended_action: Notify project finance lenders proactively with a detailed explanation before engaging GACA formally; propose a joint working group with GACA to assess cost-sharing for environmental compliance rather than immediate dispute filing.
estimated_resolution_timeline: GACA working group: 90 days; formal dispute: 18–24 months with lender default risk

matter_name: Tamam Home Furniture — Sharjah Lease Dispute
key_legal_issues: Sharjah City Centre is seeking to increase Tamam's annual rent from AED 380K to AED 520K upon lease renewal — a 37% increase Tamam rejects as commercially unviable
The existing lease contains a rent review clause permitting increases up to 15% annually — the landlord's 37% demand exceeds the contractually permitted cap
Tamam has invested AED 680K in showroom fit-out — walking away from the lease would crystallize this as a sunk cost, reducing Tamam's negotiating leverage
Sharjah Rent Disputes Committee has jurisdiction for commercial lease disputes — the DIFC clause in Tamam's lease may not be enforceable for a Sharjah-located property
recommended_action: File Sharjah Rent Disputes Committee application citing the 15% contractual cap; simultaneously negotiate lease transfer to a smaller showroom unit at the same mall to reduce rental commitment.
estimated_resolution_timeline: Rent Disputes Committee: 60–90 days; lease negotiation: parallel track

matter_name: Hilal Healthcare Staffing — DHA Licensing Review
key_legal_issues: DHA has initiated a review of Hilal's staffing license following a patient complaint against a placed nurse whose DHA license had lapsed by 14 days at the time of the incident
Hilal's license monitoring system failed to flag the nurse's license expiry — DHA is holding Hilal responsible for the placement of an unlicensed practitioner
DHA's potential sanction includes suspension of Hilal's staffing license for 90 days — 680 placements with 8 hospital clients would need to be transferred to alternative staffing arrangements
The hospital client involved in the incident has separately threatened legal action for breach of the staffing agreement warranty of licensure compliance
recommended_action: Immediately implement automated DHA license monitoring system and submit evidence to DHA within 30 days; negotiate directly with the hospital client for a goodwill payment of AED 80K to prevent formal legal action.
estimated_resolution_timeline: DHA review: 60 days; hospital client claim: 90 days

matter_name: Al Rawabi Specialty Chemicals — MEW Environmental Breach

parties.party_a: Al Rawabi Specialty Chemicals Co. WLL
parties.respondent: Ministry of Electricity and Water Kuwait

key_legal_issues: MEW inspection found chemical storage containers with inadequate secondary containment within 80 meters of a KOC pipeline easement — Al Rawabi has received a KWD 85K fine and 30-day remediation order
The secondary containment deficiency arose from a facility expansion that was not submitted to MEW for approval — unpermitted expansion creates additional regulatory exposure
KOC has been notified of the MEW finding and is reviewing whether Al Rawabi's vendor status is affected — KOC vendor review could jeopardize KWD 4.8M in annual revenue
Remediation within 30 days requires construction of secondary containment bunds — planning permission from Ahmadi Governorate is a prerequisite that typically takes 45 days
recommended_action: Apply immediately for Ahmadi Governorate emergency planning permission citing the MEW compliance deadline; simultaneously notify KOC proactively with a detailed remediation plan to protect vendor status.
estimated_resolution_timeline: MEW remediation: 45 days with emergency planning; KOC vendor review: 30 days

matter_name: Marsa Ports Logistics — JAFZA Customs Broker License
key_legal_issues: Dubai Customs has downgraded Marsa's preferred customs broker status following 3 late declaration filings in Q3 2023 — the downgrade increases customs clearance processing time from 2 hours to 8 hours
The 3 late filings were caused by Dubai Customs' own e-clearance system outage — Marsa has documentary evidence but Dubai Customs' SLA excludes system outage periods from the compliance clock
88% of Marsa's clients have SLA commitments requiring 4-hour customs clearance — the downgrade directly triggers SLA breaches across the client portfolio
Dubai Customs' preferred broker reinstatement process requires 6 months of clean filing history — 6 months of SLA breach exposure is commercially unacceptable
recommended_action: File formal reconsideration application with Dubai Customs Director-General with documented e-clearance outage evidence; simultaneously notify all 340 clients of temporary clearance time extension with force majeure framing.
estimated_resolution_timeline: Dubai Customs reconsideration: 30–45 days; SLA breach exposure: ongoing until reinstatement

matter_name: Ithmar Education Group — MOE Acquisition Approval
key_legal_issues: Saudi MOE approval is required for any change of control in a licensed private school operator — the approval application was filed 4 months ago with no response beyond an acknowledgment
MOE's internal review process does not have a statutory response deadline — the absence of a timeline creates closing uncertainty for buyer and seller
The SPA's longstop date for MOE approval is 6 months from signing — if approval is not received within 30 days the longstop triggers and buyer has termination rights
One of 9 schools has a lease agreement with a municipality entity that contains a change-of-control consent requirement — the municipality has not been approached for consent
recommended_action: Escalate MOE application through Ministry liaison at Deputy Minister level; immediately approach municipality for lease consent in parallel; request SPA longstop extension of 3 months from buyer as a protective measure.
estimated_resolution_timeline: MOE approval: 60–90 days with escalation; longstop risk: within 30 days

matter_name: Rawabet Healthcare Network — Insurance Panel Dispute
key_legal_issues: Bupa Arabia has issued notice of removal of Rawabet from its approved healthcare provider panel effective 60 days — removal would eliminate 28% of Rawabet's annual revenue
Bupa cites Rawabet's failure to implement the mandatory electronic prior authorization system by the CCHI-mandated deadline as grounds for removal
Rawabet argues it received the CCHI deadline extension communication that Bupa is denying — the communication record is disputed
Panel removal without the contractually required 6-month notice period constitutes a breach — Rawabet's contract contains a minimum notice provision that Bupa's 60-day notice does not satisfy
recommended_action: Immediately file formal dispute under the panel agreement notice provision; simultaneously accelerate e-prior authorization system implementation to demonstrate compliance before the 60-day removal date.
estimated_resolution_timeline: Mediation: 45 days; panel removal risk: within 60 days if not resolved

matter_name: Midan Sports and Wellness — Lease Assignment Dispute
key_legal_issues: Riyadh Mall has refused consent for Midan's assignment of 2 gym leases to the incoming PE buyer — mall consent is required under the lease assignment clause
Mall management is demanding a SAR 1.2M lease assignment fee not contemplated in the original lease — Midan disputes any assignment fee obligation
PE buyer's acquisition is conditional on successful lease assignment — failure to obtain consent would result in deal collapse
Saudi Commercial Court has precedent for voiding unreasonable assignment consent refusals — Midan's legal position is arguable but litigation timeline is incompatible with the deal closing schedule
recommended_action: Negotiate a SAR 400K assignment fee as a commercial resolution to avoid litigation; structure as a one-time lease amendment payment rather than an assignment fee to protect future assignments.
estimated_resolution_timeline: Commercial resolution: 30 days; litigation: 12–18 months — incompatible with deal timeline

matter_name: Durra Healthcare Analytics — MOH Data Contract Review
key_legal_issues: MOH is seeking to introduce data sovereignty provisions into the existing analytics platform contract requiring all patient data to be stored on MOH-controlled servers
Durra's current architecture uses a cloud-based platform — compliance with the new data sovereignty requirement would require a KWD 480K infrastructure rebuild not contemplated in the contract
MOH argues the data sovereignty requirement is a regulatory obligation under the Kuwait Data Protection Law that overrides the contractual data processing arrangements
Durra's contract does not contain a change-in-law cost allocation mechanism — the party bearing the rebuilding cost is disputed
recommended_action: Propose a hybrid architecture that meets MOH data residency requirements at KWD 180K cost — significantly below the full rebuild cost; seek a 50% cost-sharing mechanism from MOH as a change-in-law accommodation.
estimated_resolution_timeline: Commercial resolution: 60 days; technical implementation: 90 days

matter_name: Andalus Catering Services — Post-Acquisition Shareholder Dispute

parties.party_b: Ibrahim and Khalid Al Zahrawi (minority shareholders)

key_legal_issues: Two minority siblings holding a combined 30% stake are challenging the acquisition price in commercial court claiming they were not properly consulted during the sale process
Saudi Companies Law requires a shareholders meeting to approve a majority share transfer — the meeting minutes provided by seller show only 60% attendance, creating a procedural validity question
Minority shareholders are seeking a judicial valuation at SAR 240M against the SAR 200M agreed EV — the SAR 40M gap is the subject of the dispute
The acquisition completion accounts have not been finalized — minority shareholders are using this as additional leverage to delay completion account sign-off
recommended_action: Engage a respected Saudi tribal mediator to facilitate a family resolution before the commercial court case advances; offer a SAR 12M price increase to resolve the minority claim and avoid judicial valuation risk.
estimated_resolution_timeline: Mediated resolution: 60–90 days; commercial court judgment: 18–24 months

matter_name: Barq EV Charging Network — RTA Compliance Dispute
key_legal_issues: RTA has issued a notice of non-compliance with EV charger safety standards for 48 of 340 charging points — compliance requires AED 2.8M in hardware replacement
The safety standard was updated in 2023 after Barq's charger procurement — Barq argues the standard change requires a transition period under RTA's own implementation guidelines
RTA's notice requires decommissioning of non-compliant chargers within 30 days — decommissioning 48 of 340 chargers reduces network utilization revenue by 14%
Emaar and MAF partnership agreements require Barq to maintain RTA-compliant chargers — failure to comply risks partnership agreement termination
recommended_action: Apply for RTA 90-day compliance transition period citing 2023 standard change timing; immediately notify Emaar and MAF proactively with compliance timeline to protect partnership agreements.
estimated_resolution_timeline: RTA transition period application: 30 days; hardware replacement: 90 days

matter_name: Noor Al Bait Smart Home — IP Assignment Dispute

parties.party_a: Noor Al Bait Smart Home Co.

key_legal_issues: Former integration partner Al Mukhtar is claiming co-ownership of 3 home automation system modules developed during a 2021 joint development agreement

The joint development agreement's IP ownership clause is ambiguous — it assigns IP to the commissioning party for bespoke developments but does not clearly define which modules were bespoke versus standard
Noor Al Bait has 340 villa installations using the disputed modules — any injunction on the IP would affect all installed systems
Al Mukhtar has filed a claim with the Saudi commercial court — the claim amount is SAR 4.8M representing a royalty calculation on all 340 installations
recommended_action: Commission independent technical expert to categorize each module as bespoke or standard under the JDA definition; negotiate lump-sum settlement of SAR 800K to clear all claims before new platform development proceeds.
estimated_resolution_timeline: Technical expert review: 45 days; settlement: 60–90 days; litigation: 18 months

matter_name: Rawasi Digital Advertising — Client Data Dispute
key_legal_issues: Departing CDO has taken copies of Rawasi's proprietary Arabic audience segmentation data to a competitor — Rawasi is seeking an urgent injunction and damages
The CDO's employment contract contains a confidentiality clause but no explicit data ownership provision for data developed during employment
The DIFC Employment Law confidentiality provisions are narrower than Rawasi's contractual clause — extent of enforceable protection is uncertain
Rawasi's AED 280M managed spend business relies on the audience segmentation data as its primary differentiator — ongoing competitive damage is ongoing and unquantified
recommended_action: File DIFC Court urgent injunction application within 48 hours; simultaneously commission forensic IT analysis of data exfiltration scope; add explicit data ownership and IP assignment clauses to all future employment contracts.
estimated_resolution_timeline: Urgent injunction: 5–7 days; full arbitration: 12–18 months

matter_name: Gulf Bridge Telecom Infrastructure — CITRA Regulatory Review

parties.party_a: Gulf Bridge Telecom Infrastructure Co. WLL
parties.respondent: Communications and Information Technology Regulatory Authority

key_legal_issues: CITRA has initiated a review of Gulf Bridge's tower sharing agreements alleging that the pricing methodology discriminates against Ooredoo Kuwait relative to Zain — CITRA's equal access mandate requires identical pricing for equivalent services
Gulf Bridge's pricing reflects different infrastructure upgrade contributions by the two operators — Zain contributed KWD 1.8M to tower upgrades that Ooredoo did not participate in
CITRA's equal access regulations do not explicitly address differential pricing for differential investment contributions — the regulatory gap is being interpreted against Gulf Bridge
CITRA's potential remedy includes mandated price equalization and retroactive rebates — estimated exposure of KWD 2.4M in retroactive Ooredoo rebates
recommended_action: Submit formal CITRA representation with documented Zain infrastructure contribution justification; propose a prospective pricing equalization that does not trigger retroactive rebates.
estimated_resolution_timeline: CITRA review: 90–120 days; retroactive rebate risk: material if not proactively justified

matter_name: Masaar AgriTech — MOCCAE Incentive Dispute

parties.respondent: Ministry of Climate Change and Environment

key_legal_issues: MOCCAE has declined to approve Masaar's AED 12M food security incentive application citing failure to meet the 90% UAE-origin produce requirement — Masaar's seeds are imported from Netherlands

Masaar argues that the UAE-origin requirement applies to the growing location, not the seed origin — MOCCAE's interpretation requires seed origin to also be UAE, which is technically impossible for leafy green varieties
The incentive program guidelines were published with ambiguous language on the origin requirement — Masaar relied on an informal MOCCAE official interpretation that is now disputed
Masaar has structured its second facility business case around the MOCCAE incentive — denial creates a SAR 12M funding gap that jeopardizes the expansion
recommended_action: File formal MOCCAE appeal with legal opinion on UAE-origin requirement interpretation; engage UAE AgriTech industry association to support a clarification request to MOCCAE on seed origin policy.
estimated_resolution_timeline: MOCCAE appeal: 60–90 days; policy clarification: 120 days

matter_name: Jabal Al Noor Mining Services — Ma'aden Contract Extension

parties.party_a: Jabal Al Noor Mining Services Co.

key_legal_issues: Ma'aden has tendered the drilling services contract renewal competitively rather than extending directly with Jabal Al Noor — the incumbency advantage was not protected by the expiring contract
Jabal Al Noor's existing drill rig acquisition financing is contingent on Ma'aden contract renewal — if the tender is lost, project finance covenants are breached
Ma'aden's tender specification requires MISA Level 3 mining certification that Jabal Al Noor does not currently hold — the certification was not previously required under the existing contract
Jabal Al Noor has submitted a bid that is 8% above the competitor estimate — price competitiveness is a concern given the new MISA certification requirement disadvantage
recommended_action: Apply for MISA Level 3 certification on an expedited basis; negotiate a bridging arrangement with Ma'aden for a 6-month extension of current contract pending certification; simultaneously notify project finance lenders of the covenant risk.
estimated_resolution_timeline: MISA certification: 4–6 months; Ma'aden extension negotiation: 30 days

matter_name: Baraem Childcare Centers — MOE License Transfer
key_legal_issues: Saudi MOE nursery license is issued in the personal name of the founder — transfer of the license to a corporate buyer is not a standard MOE process and requires individual assessment
MOE has informally indicated that the nursery license may need to be surrendered and reapplied for by the new owner — reapplication process takes 8–12 months and does not guarantee approval
The SPA is structured as a share purchase — MOE may not recognize that a share purchase changes the licensee, or may treat it as a change of control requiring MOE approval
22 centers operate on the basis of the single founder license — any gap in license continuity would require immediate operational suspension of all 22 centers
recommended_action: Obtain formal MOE written guidance on share purchase versus asset purchase treatment before proceeding; restructure as an asset purchase with license reapplication timeline built into the completion conditions.
estimated_resolution_timeline: MOE guidance: 30–45 days; license reapplication: 8–12 months post-closing

matter_name: Naseem Marine Services — P&I Club Dispute
key_legal_issues: P&I Club is disputing coverage for a KWD 280K third-party liability claim arising from a vessel collision in Kuwaiti territorial waters — Club argues the vessel was operating outside its declared trade route
Naseem's certificate of entry declares Kuwait and offshore Gulf operations — the collision occurred in a Kuwait Oil Company exclusion zone not disclosed to the P&I Club at entry
P&I Club's right to void coverage for material non-disclosure is broad under English law — the governing law of the P&I entry is English, not Kuwaiti
KOC exclusion zone operation was required under the KOC framework agreement — Naseem argues the non-disclosure was inadvertent given the operational necessity
recommended_action: Retain English marine law counsel immediately; argue inadvertent non-disclosure with documentation of KOC operational necessity; negotiate ex-gratia settlement with P&I Club at 60% of claim to avoid voiding coverage.
estimated_resolution_timeline: P&I negotiation: 90 days; London arbitration if not settled: 18–24 months

matter_name: Raha Water Parks Group — Abu Dhabi Municipality Lease Dispute
key_legal_issues: Abu Dhabi municipality has issued notice to increase annual land lease from AED 2.8M to AED 4.4M upon renewal — a 57% increase Raha rejects as inconsistent with the lease escalation mechanism
The lease escalation mechanism is based on Abu Dhabi CPI — CPI-based increase would yield only AED 3.1M, not AED 4.4M
Municipality argues that the land has been reclassified to a tourism zone with higher land use fees — the reclassification occurred post-execution and was not provided for in the lease
Raha's park operations depend on the Abu Dhabi site for 58% of revenue — losing the lease would be existential for the business
recommended_action: Accept CPI-based increase of AED 3.1M as the contractually correct calculation; formally reject AED 4.4M through written correspondence citing lease mechanism; engage Abu Dhabi Tourism Authority as a mediating party given the tourism zone reclassification.
estimated_resolution_timeline: Municipality negotiation: 60 days; formal dispute: 12–18 months

matter_name: Tamkin Digital Government Solutions — Data Breach Notification
key_legal_issues: A cyber incident at Tamkin exposed citizen transaction data from 3 government ministry clients — NCA notification was made 96 hours after detection, exceeding the mandatory 72-hour notification window
NCA is investigating whether the late notification constitutes a PDPL breach subject to SAR 5M administrative fine
Two of three affected government ministry clients have issued breach notices under their data processing agreements — potential contractual damages exposure of SAR 8M
Tamkin's directors face personal liability under PDPL Article 38 for failure to implement adequate data protection measures — criminal prosecution risk is non-zero
recommended_action: Submit comprehensive NCA incident report immediately with full root cause analysis and remediation plan; retain criminal defense counsel for directors as a precautionary measure; engage government ministry clients for confidential breach resolution.
estimated_resolution_timeline: NCA investigation: 90–120 days; ministry client claims: 60 days; criminal risk assessment: immediate

matter_name: Wajd Digital Media Group — GCAM Content Takedown
key_legal_issues: GCAM has issued a takedown order for 3 YouTube channels totalling 12M subscribers citing content standard violations — the content in question is 18-month-old comedy programming
Wajd disputes that the referenced content violates current GCAM standards — the content was approved under 2022 guidelines that were subsequently revised
GCAM's retroactive enforcement against previously compliant content raises a legitimate rule of law argument — enforcement against compliant historical content is legally contestable
Revenue impact of the 3-channel takedown is SAR 8.4M annually — growth equity investor default clauses may be triggered if revenue falls below the covenant threshold
recommended_action: File urgent GCAM appeal with documentation of 2022 compliance approval; engage Saudi media law counsel to challenge retroactive enforcement; notify investors proactively with revenue impact quantification before covenant review date.
estimated_resolution_timeline: GCAM appeal: 30–45 days; court challenge if rejected: 12 months; investor covenant: immediate management

matter_name: Majd Cybersecurity Consulting — NCA Contract Dispute
key_legal_issues: Jawazat has withheld SAR 3.2M in cybersecurity audit fees citing a deliverable quality dispute — Majd disputes the quality assessment basis used by Jawazat's internal technical review team

Jawazat's technical review team applied NIST CSF evaluation criteria not specified in the original SOW — Majd's deliverables were prepared against NCA Essential Cybersecurity Controls as contractually specified
Government payment withholding requires mandatory ministry dispute committee process before court — Majd has not yet initiated this process
CSO key person who managed the Jawazat relationship has resigned — his departure weakens the client relationship for the dispute resolution process
recommended_action: File ministry dispute committee application immediately; retain the departed CSO as a paid consultant specifically for the dispute resolution process to preserve the client relationship context.
estimated_resolution_timeline: Ministry dispute committee: 6–9 months; commercial court escalation: 18–24 months

matter_name: Ihsan Social Enterprise Fund — GAZT Tax Dispute

parties.respondent: General Authority of Zakat and Tax

key_legal_issues: GAZT is challenging Ihsan's claim to the 30% impact investment tax deduction on SAR 22M in LP commitments — GAZT argues that 4 of 14 investees do not qualify as social enterprises under GAZT's narrow definition

The GAZT social enterprise definition requires a primary social purpose test — 4 investees have commercial revenue exceeding 50% of total income, which GAZT interprets as failing the primary social purpose test
Ihsan's LPs have claimed the deduction on their 2022 tax returns — if GAZT prevails, each LP faces a SAR 1.6M average tax reassessment

GAZT's position creates a retroactive uncertainty for all impact investment vehicles in Saudi Arabia — the fund management industry has flagged this as a systemic issue requiring GAZT policy clarification
recommended_action: File formal GAZT objection with legal opinion on social enterprise definition interpretation; coordinate with NCSC to seek ministry-level clarification on the qualifying criteria before the LP reassessment notices are issued.
estimated_resolution_timeline: GAZT objection: 90 days; LP reassessment risk: within 6 months if not resolved
